contextSummary: Product-analytics app chrome built directly on Menu: the account/org/project switcher and help menus use a Backdrop layer, nested SubmenuRoots (All projects / All organizations), render-prop triggers into PostHog's ButtonPrimitive, and a scroll-shadow popup capped by --available-height; a separate 'quill' package wraps full dropdown-menu + menubar primitives.
rankRationale: The richest direct product usage found: PostHog's account/org/project switcher is hand-built on Menu with a Backdrop layer (the only Backdrop sighting in the corpus), two nested SubmenuRoots, render-prop triggers into their ButtonPrimitive, and a popup capped by the --available-height CSS variable behind scroll shadows. Feeds the Behavior (submenus, backdrop), Prop-level (render, Backdrop), and CSS-variables sections. Recomposition story: open account menu -> ArrowDown through items -> ArrowRight into 'All projects' submenu -> select a project.

contextSummary: Next.js DevTools dev-overlay segment explorer: each route-segment row gets a boundary-override menu with `modal={false}`, a trigger that is disabled when the segment has no boundary, and `Menu.Portal container={shadowRoot}` so the popup stays inside the overlay's shadow DOM.
rankRationale: Menu running inside every Next.js developer's dev overlay: modal={false} so the page stays interactive, Menu.Portal container={shadowRoot} portalling into the overlay's shadow root, and a trigger that is disabled until the hovered route segment has a boundary. The single best citation for the Portal container prop and non-modal menus in constrained embedding environments. Feeds Prop-level guidance (modal, container, disabled) and an 'embedding in shadow DOM' pitfall note.

contextSummary: Playroom's frame/settings menus: one Menu.tsx drives top-level menus and submenus, with closeOnClick={false} on checkbox/radio settings items and onOpenChangeComplete to reset submenu state after the close animation.
rankRationale: A settings/checkbox menu done right in a widely-used dev tool: closeOnClick={false} keeps the menu open while toggling CheckboxItems/RadioItems, closeOnClick is also suppressed on submenu triggers via one shared MenuItem abstraction, and onOpenChangeComplete resets submenu state only after the exit animation. Feeds prop-level guidance for closeOnClick and onOpenChangeComplete, and the checkbox-settings-menu use case story: open -> toggle two checkboxes without the menu closing -> Escape.

contextSummary: Oxide cloud console UI lib: DropdownMenu re-exports Root with a modal={false} default (comment: prevent scroll locking), parses a semantic anchor="bottom end" string into side/align/offsets, and backs table row-action menus across the console.
rankRationale: A production cloud console (Oxide) that wraps Menu with modal={false} as the default and a code comment explaining why (prevent scroll locking), plus a semantic anchor="bottom end" string API parsed into side/align/offsets. The clearest evidence that row-action menus in data tables want non-modal behavior. MPL-2.0 so code is reusable. Feeds When-to-use (row actions), Prop-level (modal, side/align), and a decision-log-style note on scroll locking.

contextSummary: cmux marketing-site split Download CTA: the left zone is a plain link, the right caret zone is a Menu.Trigger opening the platform list (items render as i18n Links, waitlist platforms grouped under a GroupLabel); selecting a waitlist item opens a dialog deferred by requestAnimationFrame so the same pointer gesture doesn't dismiss it.
rankRationale: A marketing split-button CTA: 'Download for Mac' link + caret Menu.Trigger listing platforms, items rendered as framework Links via the render prop, a Group/GroupLabel for waitlist platforms, and an exceptional documented workaround - opening a follow-up dialog inside requestAnimationFrame so the same pointer gesture that selected the item doesn't outside-press-dismiss the dialog. Feeds the split-button use case and a menu-to-dialog-handoff pitfall. Recomposition: click caret -> arrow to a waitlist platform -> select -> dialog opens and stays.

contextSummary: Cloudflare's Kumo design system: a primitives re-export layer plus a Dropdown composite that promotes Menu.LinkItem for navigation (JSDoc deprecates href-on-Item), danger-styled items, and a container-aware Portal.
rankRationale: Cloudflare's Kumo design system Dropdown composite documents an API migration in JSDoc: href-on-Item is deprecated in favor of Menu.LinkItem for navigation, alongside danger-styled items and a container-aware Portal. Best-in-corpus evidence for when to use LinkItem vs Item, exactly the prop-level 'use X when, because' guidance the docs want. MIT and actively maintained.

contextSummary: Vocs docs framework MobileNav: Menu.RadioGroup marks the current page, RadioItems render as links grouped into sections, with data-checked styling as the selection indicator.
rankRationale: Vocs (docs framework powering viem/wagmi docs) uses Menu.RadioGroup as a current-page indicator in its mobile nav: RadioItems render as links with data-checked styling marking the active route, grouped sections via Group/GroupLabel. A distinct semantic archetype - radio state as selection display rather than a setting - feeding the RadioGroup/RadioItem guidance and a navigation-menu-vs-menu boundary note.

contextSummary: Gutenberg's next-gen packages/ui AlertDialog Storybook story embeds a controlled Menu ('Actions' -> Delete opens an AlertDialog; onConfirm closes the menu), demonstrating menu-to-dialog composition in WordPress's Base UI-based design system.
rankRationale: WordPress's next-gen packages/ui uses Base UI, and its AlertDialog Storybook story embeds a controlled Menu: 'Actions' menu where Delete opens an AlertDialog and onConfirm closes the menu via setMenuOpen(false). A canonical menu-item-opens-confirm-dialog composition from one of the largest OSS projects adopting Base UI. Feeds the controlled-Root guidance and an overlay-composition cluster note (menu + alert-dialog).

contextSummary: Menubar recipe by a Material UI maintainer: Base UI Menubar + Menu with every item rendered into a Material UI MenuItem via render props (plus a SKILL.md teaching agents the integration).
rankRationale: A Material UI maintainer's recipe collection: Base UI Menubar + Menu.Root per menu, with every Menu.Item rendered into a Material UI MenuItem via the render prop (plus an agent-facing SKILL.md describing the pattern). The strongest render-prop interop evidence in the corpus - Base UI behavior, third-party design language - and the only ranked Menubar composition. Feeds the render-prop section and a menubar cross-reference.

contextSummary: FileContextMenu composes ContextMenu.Root/Trigger with Menu.Portal/Positioner/Popup/Item parts (cross-component part interop); the browser app also uses raw Menu parts for agent-selection menus and ships a stage-ui Menu wrapper.
rankRationale: FileContextMenu composes ContextMenu.Root/Trigger with Menu.Portal/Positioner/Popup/Item - live proof that context-menu shares menu's part vocabulary and the parts interoperate; sibling files use raw Menu parts for agent-selection menus in the same app. Feeds the menu/context-menu cluster brief (when the only difference is the trigger gesture) and the anatomy section's part-interop note.

contextSummary: Propel design system's CustomMenu wraps Menu (old package name) behind a simplified API that exposes openOnHover; used across the project-management app for work-item and board row actions.
rankRationale: Plane (54k-star project-management tool) wraps Menu in its Propel design system as CustomMenu, exposing openOnHover through the wrapper API and serving row/board item actions across the product - large-scale evidence of the wrapper-with-escape-hatches pattern on the historical package name. Feeds When-to-use (row actions at scale) and openOnHover prop guidance. AGPL, so link-only.

contextSummary: dify-ui package DropdownMenu: full-part wrapper including a Menu.LinkItem sub-wrapper with a closeOnClick default for navigation entries in the LLM-app platform.
rankRationale: The highest-starred adopter found (148k): dify-ui's DropdownMenu is a full-part wrapper whose LinkItem sub-component defaults closeOnClick for navigation entries. Confirms the LinkItem-for-navigation idiom (independently of Kumo) at the largest possible scale. Feeds LinkItem guidance and the real-world-adoption narrative. Custom license, link-only.

contextSummary: T3 code product web app: full shadcn-style wrapper (data-slot conventions) including a createHandle re-export, derived from the Coss UI registry.
rankRationale: A shipped MIT product wrapper (derived from the Coss UI registry, menu-011) that re-exports Menu.createHandle alongside the full part set with data-slot conventions - the ranked representative for detached-trigger menu handles appearing in real apps, a 1.x API with almost no visibility elsewhere. Feeds the createHandle prop/decision-log section; coss (origin, AGPL) and kaneo (menu-018) corroborate.

contextSummary: Animate UI registry 'base menu' primitive: every Menu part (incl. Arrow) wrapped with motion/react springs, animating the popup via render props and AnimatePresence instead of CSS transitions.
rankRationale: Animate UI's base-menu registry primitive wraps every Menu part (including the rarely-seen Arrow) with motion/react springs, driving popup enter/exit through AnimatePresence and render props instead of CSS starting/ending styles. The definitive JS-animation-integration example for the docs' Animation section, and a registry distributed to end users as copy-paste code.

contextSummary: curl.md docs site mobile outline menu: modal={false} with Positioner anchor={mobileOutlineTriggerRef} (anchored to an external ref, not the trigger), items render <a href> with closeOnClick and data-[active] styling.
rankRationale: curl.md's mobile docs outline: Menu with modal={false} and Positioner anchor={mobileOutlineTriggerRef} - anchoring the popup to an external element ref rather than the trigger - with items rendered as plain <a href> + closeOnClick and data-[active] styling. The only anchor-ref sighting in the corpus; feeds Positioner.anchor prop guidance and the docs-outline navigation use case.
